mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and moderate inflation indicate robust consumer demand without pricing power erosion, which directly boosts corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed cases, or interaction term IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC over any 18-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion, which raises baseline corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple quarters, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which prompts immediate risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle downturns, with forward returns remaining positive in over 60% of triggered instances over a 24-month rolling evaluation.
